question_text: Name one everyday use of an electromagnet.
correct_answer: Any one valid example from: doorbells, electric motors, MRI scanners, cranes, relays, or speakers
explanation: Electromagnets are magnets created by electric current flowing through a coil of wire. They are very useful in everyday life because we can turn them on and off by controlling the electric current. Common examples include doorbells that use electromagnets to ring the bell, electric motors in appliances, and cranes that use electromagnets to lift metal objects.
